text: In the right retro-rectal space, the posterior rectal sheath is visible at the bottom of the screen, and the rectus muscle is at the top. The surgeon dissects by dividing the medial contribution of the posterior rectal sheath to the linea alba, then crosses over to the contralateral side to continue the dissection. The umbilical hernia contents are reduced, and the edges of the defects are clearly identified for repair.

The surgeon continues to dissect the midline structures to unify the retro-rectal spaces on both sides. All visible peritoneal violations are meticulously closed to prevent bowel herniation into the pre-peritoneal space.
The surgeon performs blunt dissection to separate the plane between the transverse abdominal muscle and the underlying fascia and peritoneum, continuing the ETAR procedure.

The surgeon closes the left lateral abdominal wall defect by suturing the muscle fibers together. The midline structures are closed by suturing the edges of the rectus muscle on both sides to close the midline defects. A folded mesh is unfolded and positioned in the retro-rectal space beneath the rectus muscle and transverse abdominis.
